wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Getting Started section focused on Laravel Assistant project setup and initial development workflow. Explain the project's purpose as an AI-powered Laravel 13 application scaffold, its key features including multi-provider AI integration and agent-based development. Document the complete installation process from prerequisites through initial setup, environment configuration, and development server startup. Include step-by-step instructions for Laravel Boost installation and AI agent integration. Provide practical examples demonstrating common development scenarios and explain the relationship between Laravel conventions and AI-assisted development. Use terminology consistent with the Laravel ecosystem and AI development tools. Address both beginners new to Laravel and experienced developers transitioning to AI-enhanced workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Laravel Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the core Laravel architecture components. Explain the MVC pattern implementation, service container organization, and dependency injection principles. Document the application bootstrap process starting from bootstrap/app.php, including routing configuration, middleware setup, and exception handling. Detail the service provider registration mechanism and how AppServiceProvider integrates with the Laravel container. Cover the relationship between configuration files and runtime application behavior. Include practical examples of how controllers, models, and services interact within the Laravel framework. Address the separation of concerns, naming conventions, and Laravel's convention-over-configuration philosophy. Provide code examples showing typical request-response cycles and component interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AI Configuration focused on the multi-provider setup and environment management. Document the config/ai.php structure including default provider assignments for different AI modalities (text, images, audio, embeddings). Explain the provider configuration array with driver specifications, API keys, URLs, and provider-specific parameters. Detail environment variable management using env() function calls and their security implications. Cover caching configuration for embeddings and other AI operations. Include practical examples of configuring different providers like Anthropic, Gemini, OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, and local providers like Ollama. Document provider selection strategies, fallback mechanisms, and performance optimization techniques. Address security considerations for API key management and environment configuration best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Controllers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Controllers focused on the Laravel controller implementation within the assistant project. Explain the abstract Controller base class structure, controller inheritance patterns, and HTTP request handling conventions. Document controller method naming conventions, parameter binding, and response formatting. Detail how controllers interact with the AI system for agent-based workflows, including request processing for AI-enabled endpoints. Include practical examples demonstrating controller creation, route-to-controller mapping, and dependency injection patterns. Explain middleware integration, validation handling, and error response formatting. Address Laravel controller best practices, performance optimization, and security considerations. Document the relationship between traditional controllers and AI-enhanced request processing patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema documentation for the Laravel Assistant application. Detail the complete database structure including users table with authentication fields, password reset tokens, sessions table for user session management, agent conversations table for AI interaction tracking, cache table for application caching, and jobs table for background processing. Document field definitions, data types, primary keys, foreign keys, unique constraints, and indexes. Explain the relationships between tables and how they support the application's core functionality. Include database schema diagrams showing entity relationships and data flow patterns. Document the rationale behind each table design decision and how they align with Laravel's authentication and session management patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pest Framework Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Pest Framework Integration focused on the Pest PHP testing framework setup and configuration within Laravel. Explain the Pest framework initialization, global test configuration, and TestCase base class setup. Document the Pest autoloading mechanism, test discovery patterns, and framework-specific Laravel integration points. Include practical examples of Pest configuration options, custom assertions, and helper functions. Explain how Pest integrates with Laravel's service container, database testing capabilities, and mocking frameworks. Address Pest-specific testing patterns, performance optimization, and debugging techniques. Make content accessible to developers new to Pest while providing advanced configuration options for experienced users.

wiki_catalogs.name: Asset Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Asset Pipeline focused on Vite build system configuration and asset management. Explain the Vite plugin setup including laravel-vite-plugin integration and @tailwindcss/vite configuration. Document the build process, asset compilation workflow, and development server configuration. Include concrete examples of asset bundling, hot module replacement, and production optimization. Detail the JavaScript entry point configuration, CSS preprocessing setup, and file watching patterns. Address build performance optimization, asset fingerprinting, and CDN integration strategies. Explain the relationship between Vite configuration and Laravel's asset serving mechanisms. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common build issues and development server problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Skill Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Skill Management System focused on Laravel Boost's skill-based AI assistance framework. Thoroughly explain how skills are activated, configured, and utilized within the Laravel ecosystem. Document the three core skills: laravel-best-practices for PHP code patterns and architecture, pest-testing for testing workflows, and tailwindcss-development for frontend styling. Include concrete examples of skill activation triggers, skill-specific rule sets, and how each skill integrates with Laravel development patterns. Explain the skill dependency system, rule-based enforcement, and how skills complement Laravel's built-in development tools. Address skill customization, rule modification, and best practices for skill activation timing. Provide troubleshooting guidance for skill conflicts and optimization strategies for skill performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Skill System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Skill System component. Document the three core skills available: Laravel Best Practices, Pest Testing, and Tailwind CSS Development. Explain how each skill provides specialized AI functionality for specific development domains. Detail the skill activation triggers, configuration options, and integration patterns. Cover the rule-based guidance system within each skill, showing how they provide context-aware recommendations and code generation. Include practical examples of skill usage scenarios, activation commands, and how skills interact with the Laravel Boost framework. Document skill-specific best practices, common use cases, and troubleshooting approaches. Address skill customization, extension possibilities, and how new skills can be developed following the established patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Production Environment Setup focused on configuring Laravel Assistant for production deployment. Explain environment variable management, application configuration for production, and security hardening measures. Document the APP_ENV setting, APP_DEBUG configuration, encryption key setup, and timezone configuration. Detail database connection optimization, cache configuration for production, and session management strategies. Include practical examples of .env.production file setup, security headers configuration, and performance optimization settings. Address common configuration pitfalls and their solutions. Explain the relationship between development and production configurations, including environment-specific settings and security considerations.

Include practical examples of common configuration scenarios such as development vs production environments, custom encryption keys, and locale-specific settings. Address security considerations for sensitive configuration values, environment variable management, and best practices for application customization. Explain how these settings affect application behavior and provide troubleshooting guidance for common configuration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Laravel Best Practices Skill
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Laravel Best Practices skill. Detail how this skill provides context-aware recommendations for Laravel PHP development including database performance optimization, advanced query patterns, security implementations, caching strategies, Eloquent patterns, validation approaches, configuration management, testing patterns, queue/job configurations, routing/controller patterns, HTTP client usage, events/notifications/mail handling, error handling, task scheduling, architectural decisions, migration practices, collections usage, Blade/view patterns, and coding conventions. Document the rule-based guidance system with priority ordering, consistency-first approach, and how to apply specific rules based on file types. Include practical examples of skill activation, rule selection criteria, and integration with Laravel Boost framework. Address common Laravel development scenarios and how this skill enhances code quality and performance.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for AI integration architectural patterns within the Laravel Assistant framework. wiki_catalogs.name: Agent System

wiki_catalogs.name: Models

Document best practices for test-driven development and continuous integration workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for Frontend Development focused on the modern frontend toolchain and asset management system. Thoroughly explain the Vite build configuration, JavaScript module system, and CSS preprocessing setup. Document the Tailwind CSS integration, utility-first styling approach, and responsive design patterns. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing asset compilation, component development, and build optimization. Explain the Blade templating system, template inheritance, and data passing patterns. Address frontend performance optimization, asset bundling strategies, and production build processes. Make content accessible to developers new to modern frontend tooling while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced frontend developers. Document the integration between Laravel's server-side rendering and modern client-side development workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration and Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Configuration and Customization focused on Laravel Assistant's extensive configuration options and extension points. Explain the environment-based configuration system, application settings, cache and session configuration, and database connection setup. Document the AI service configuration, provider-specific settings, and multi-provider management. Detail the customization options for agents, skills, and development workflows. Include practical examples of common configuration scenarios, environment variable management, and production deployment settings. Explain how to extend the system with custom providers, additional agent skills, and modified development workflows. Address security configuration, performance tuning, and monitoring setup. Document the relationship between configuration options and system behavior, including troubleshooting common configuration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Production
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Deployment and Production focused on deploying Laravel Assistant to production environments. Explain production environment setup, security hardening, and performance optimization strategies. Document the build and optimization process for production assets, database migration procedures, and environment configuration management. Include practical examples of deployment workflows, CI/CD integration, and monitoring setup. Detail the production asset compilation process, caching strategies, and performance monitoring approaches. Address common deployment issues, troubleshooting techniques, and maintenance procedures. Explain the relationship between development and production configurations, including environment variable management and security considerations. Document best practices for scaling, load balancing, and disaster recovery in production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ content for Laravel Assistant development and deployment issues. Document common problems encountered during installation, configuration, and development workflows. Include step-by-step solutions for environment setup issues, AI provider integration problems, and agent development challenges. Address performance troubleshooting, error resolution strategies, and debugging techniques. Explain how to diagnose and resolve common Laravel framework issues, AI service connectivity problems, and frontend build failures. Include frequently asked questions about Laravel Boost integration, agent skill usage, and AI development workflows. Document diagnostic tools, log analysis techniques, and systematic approaches to problem resolution. Provide guidance for reporting bugs, requesting features, and contributing to the project. Address both development-time and runtime troubleshooting scenarios with practical solutions and preventive measures.

wiki_items.title: Data Generation with Factories and Seeders

wiki_overview.content:
Now I'll analyze the project structure and key components to provide a comprehensive overview.

<blog>

# Laravel Assistant Project Analysis

## Project Introduction

The Laravel Assistant is a modern Laravel 13 application scaffold that serves as a foundation for building AI-powered web applications. This project demonstrates cutting-edge Laravel development practices with integrated AI capabilities, comprehensive testing infrastructure, and modern frontend tooling.

### Purpose Statement
The project provides a production-ready Laravel application template equipped with:
- AI integration capabilities through the `laravel/ai` package
- Comprehensive testing framework with Pest
- Modern frontend tooling with Vite and Tailwind CSS v4
- AI best practices and guidelines for Laravel development
- Multi-provider AI configuration support

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Demonstrate Laravel 13 best practices and modern development workflows
- Showcase AI integration patterns within Laravel applications
- Provide a robust testing foundation with Pest framework
- Implement modern frontend development with Vite and Tailwind CSS
- Establish clear coding standards and architectural guidelines

### Target Audience
- Laravel developers building AI-powered applications
- Teams adopting modern Laravel development practices
- Developers learning AI integration patterns in PHP frameworks
- Organizations seeking production-ready Laravel scaffolds

## Technical Architecture

The Laravel Assistant follows a clean, modular architecture that separates concerns effectively while maintaining simplicity for rapid development.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Laravel Application]
    B --> C[HTTP Kernel]
    C --> D[Route Dispatcher]
    D --> E[Controller Layer]
    E --> F[Business Logic]
    F --> G[Data Access Layer]
    G --> H[(Database)]
    
    I[AI Provider Layer] --> J[AI Service]
    J --> K[External APIs]
    
    L[Frontend Assets] --> M[Vite Build System]
    M --> N[Tailwind CSS v4]
    
    O[Testing Framework] --> P[Pest PHP]
    P --> Q[Unit Tests]
    P --> R[Feature Tests]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Framework Layer:**
- Laravel 13 Application Foundation
- PSR-4 Autoloading System
- Service Container and Dependency Injection
- Middleware Pipeline Architecture

**AI Integration Layer:**
- Multi-provider AI configuration system
- Provider-agnostic AI service abstraction
- Caching strategies for AI operations
- Environment-based provider selection

**Testing Infrastructure:**
- Pest PHP testing framework
- Feature and unit test organization
- Mocking and assertion patterns
- Test database management

**Frontend Development:**
- Vite build system for asset management
- Tailwind CSS v4 utility-first framework
- Modern JavaScript toolchain
- Hot module replacement for development

### Design Patterns

**Repository Pattern:** Used for data access abstraction
**Service Layer Pattern:** Encapsulates business logic
**Strategy Pattern:** Implemented for AI provider selection
**Factory Pattern:** Applied for model and test data generation

### System Relationships

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Application Core] --> B[AI Services]
    A --> C[Testing Framework]
    A --> D[Frontend Tooling]
    
    B --> E[Anthropic API]
    B --> F[OpenAI API]
    B --> G[Gemini API]
    B --> H[Other Providers]
    
    C --> I[Pest Tests]
    C --> J[PHPUnit]
    
    D --> K[Vite]
    D --> L[Tailwind CSS]
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Bootstrap Configuration** (`bootstrap/app.php`)
- Application initialization and service registration
- Routing configuration with web and console routes
- Middleware pipeline definition
- Exception handling setup

**Application Configuration** (`config/app.php`)
- Core application settings and environment detection
- Debug mode configuration
- Timezone and locale settings
- Encryption key management

**AI Configuration** (`config/ai.php`)
- Multi-provider AI service configuration
- Provider-specific API keys and endpoints
- Caching strategies for AI operations
- Default provider selection logic

### Core Modules

**User Model** (`app/Models/User.php`)
- Eloquent model with attribute casting
- Authentication and notification traits
- Factory integration for testing
- Fillable and hidden attributes

**Base Controller** (`app/Http/Controllers/Controller.php`)
- Abstract controller foundation
- Shared controller functionality
- Consistent API response patterns

**Database Migrations** (`database/migrations/`)
- User authentication tables
- Password reset tokens
- Session management
- Agent conversation tracking

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration** (`config/*.php`)
- Modular configuration organization
- Environment-specific settings
- Provider configuration management
- Cache and session settings

**Build Configuration** (`package.json`, `vite.config.js`)
- Frontend asset bundling
- Development server configuration
- Production optimization settings

### External Dependencies

**Core Laravel Dependencies:**
- `laravel/framework`: Application framework
- `laravel/ai`: AI service integration
- `laravel/tinker`: Interactive debugging

**Development Dependencies:**
- `pestphp/pest`: Modern PHP testing framework
- `laravel/pint`: Code formatting tool
- `laravel/boost`: Development assistance tools

**Frontend Dependencies:**
- `tailwindcss`: Utility-first CSS framework
- `vite`: Modern build tool
- `@tailwindcss/vite`: Tailwind integration

### Integration Points

**AI Provider Integration:**
- Multi-provider architecture with fallback support
- Environment-based configuration
- Caching layer for improved performance
- Error handling and retry mechanisms

**Testing Integration:**
- Pest framework with Laravel-specific features
- Database testing with factories
- Mocking and assertion patterns
- Continuous integration support

**Frontend Integration:**
- Vite for asset compilation
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- Hot module replacement for development
- Production optimization and minification

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Application {
        +configure()
        +withRouting()
        +withMiddleware()
        +withExceptions()
    }
    
    class UserController {
        +index()
        +show()
        +create()
        +store()
        +edit()
        +update()
        +destroy()
    }
    
    class UserModel {
        +fillable[]
        +hidden[]
        +casts()
        +notifications()
    }
    
    class AIService {
        +defaultProvider
        +providers[]
        +cacheSettings
        +execute()
    }
    
    Application --> UserController
    UserController --> UserModel
    Application --> AIService
    UserModel --> Notification
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**AI-Powered Application Framework:**
- Multi-provider AI service abstraction
- Configurable AI operations with caching
- Provider-specific optimizations
- Environment-based fallback strategies

**Modern Development Workflow:**
- Integrated testing with Pest framework
- Automated code formatting with Laravel Pint
- Development assistance with Laravel Boost
- Hot reloading during development

**Production-Ready Infrastructure:**
- Optimized asset bundling with Vite
- Modern CSS with Tailwind CSS v4
- Secure authentication with Laravel Sanctum
- Database migration management

### Implementation Highlights

**AI Configuration Management** (`config/ai.php`)
- Comprehensive provider configuration
- Environment variable integration
- Caching strategy implementation
- Multi-language and multimodal support

**Testing Infrastructure** (`tests/`)
- Feature and unit test organization
- Pest framework integration
- Factory-based data generation
- Assertion patterns and mocking

**Frontend Asset Management** (`resources/`)
- Modern CSS with Tailwind utilities
- JavaScript module system
- Blade templating engine
- Responsive design patterns

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> ApplicationStart
    ApplicationStart --> LoadingConfig
    LoadingConfig --> InitializingServices
    InitializingServices --> ConfiguringAI
    ConfiguringAI --> SettingUpTesting
    SettingUpTesting --> BuildingAssets
    BuildingAssets --> Ready
    
    Ready --> ProcessingRequest
    ProcessingRequest --> ExecutingAI
    ExecutingAI --> ReturningResponse
    ReturningResponse --> Ready
    
    Ready --> RunningTests
    RunningTests --> Ready
```

**Development Workflow State Machine:**
- Application initialization and configuration
- Service registration and dependency injection
- AI provider setup and caching configuration
- Asset compilation and optimization
- Testing execution and validation
- Production deployment preparation

**AI Integration Flow:**
- Provider selection based on configuration
- Request routing to appropriate AI service
- Response caching and optimization
- Error handling and fallback mechanisms
- Performance monitoring and metrics

Sources:
- [composer.json](composer.json)
- [package.json](package.json)
- [config/app.php](config/app.php)
- [config/ai.php](config/ai.php)
- [routes/web.php](routes/web.php)
- [app/Http/Controllers/Controller.php](app/Http/Controllers/Controller.php)
- [app/Models/User.php](app/Models/User.php)
- [database/migrations/0001_01_01_000000_create_users_table.php](database/migrations/0001_01_01_000000_create_users_table.php)
- [resources/views/welcome.blade.php](resources/views/welcome.blade.php)
- [bootstrap/app.php](bootstrap/app.php)
- [.agents/skills/laravel-best-practices/SKILL.md](.agents/skills/laravel-best-practices/SKILL.md)
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)

</blog>
